stdout:
## Success criteria from issue description - ✅ **Montgomery internals + API preservation** are implemented (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs:33-35, 105-113, 126-131, 173-190`; `crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/montgomery.rs`). - ⚠️ **Cross-verification** is strong but not exact to spec wording: exhaustive checks for `Fp<3>` and 2000-case proptests for `Fp<65537>`/`Fp<2^61-1>` are present (`mod.rs:629-760`), but there is no explicit “10,000+ random ops including Fp<3>” harness. - ❌ **Benchmark acceptance criterion is not met as evidenced in-repo**: benchmarks include naive baselines (including add/sub) (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs:139-215`), but there is no committed result/evidence or threshold enforcement for `>=3x` mul and `>=2x` add/sub speedups. - ✅ **Constant-time hot-path style** (add/sub/mul reduction) is branchless in Montgomery helpers (`montgomery.rs:74-83, 111-127`), with only compile-time-constant modulus specialization in `mul` (`mod.rs:184-189`). ## Separation of concerns - ✅ Changes are confined to `gf2-core` (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/*`, `crates/gf2-core/benches/fp_montgomery.rs`, `crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml`), with no `gf2-core -> gf2-coding` dependency introduced (`Cargo.toml` has no `gf2-coding` entry). ## Functional paradigm and performance - ✅ High-level API remains clean; low-level Montgomery path uses efficient bit/word arithmetic and reduction. - ⚠️ Performance intent is good, but required benchmark outcome proof is missing, leaving acceptance incomplete. ## Correctness - ✅ Montgomery constants and REDC behavior are well-tested (`montgomery.rs:163-425`), and field ops align with modular arithmetic checks (`mod.rs:629-760`). - ✅ BitVec tail-masking/bit numbering invariants are unaffected by this change set (no touched `bitvec` mutation logic). ## Testing - ✅ Substantial tests exist (unit + exhaustive + proptest), including edge primes and inversion checks. - ⚠️ I could not rerun cargo commands in this environment due permission denial for `cargo`; relying on provided gate status where `fmt/clippy/tests` are marked passed in context. - ❌ Acceptance still fails because benchmark success thresholds are not demonstrated. ## Unsafe isolation - ✅ No `unsafe` introduced in touched `gfp` files; unsafe isolation policy is preserved. ## Documentation - ✅ Public `Fp

` docs were updated to describe Montgomery representation and examples (`mod.rs:1-31, 42-71`). - ⚠️ Documentation/repo artifacts do not provide the required benchmark speedup evidence. ## Prior review feedback for this issue - ✅ Previously missing naive add/sub baseline appears addressed (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs:151-165, 210-214`). - ❌ Most recent substantive failure remains unresolved: required speedup thresholds are still not evidenced/enforced. ## Jit issue dependencies - ✅ Prerequisite `350bff7f` is marked `done` in provided context, and this work builds on that implementation. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
